content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of size NNN. Find the maximum value of the expression aŌłŚb+aŌłÆba * b + a - baŌłŚb+aŌłÆb where aaa and bbb are 222 distinct elements of the array.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of two lines of input.
First line will contain NNN, number of elements of the array.
Second line contains NNN space separated integers, elements of the array AAA.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line answer to the problem.

###Constraints


1ŌēżTŌēż201 \leq T \leq 201ŌēżTŌēż20
2ŌēżNŌēż1052 \leq N \leq 10^52ŌēżNŌēż105
ŌłÆ109ŌēżAiŌēż109-10^9 \leq A_i \leq 10^9ŌłÆ109ŌēżAiŌĆŗŌēż109
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N (the size of the array). The second line contains N spaceŌĆæseparated integers A1, A2, ŌĆ”, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible value of a┬Ęb + |aŌĆæb| where a and b are values of two distinct elements of the array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Case 1: Only possible pair is [2,2][2, 2][2,2] for which answer = 2ŌłŚ2+2ŌłÆ22 * 2 + 2 - 22ŌłŚ2+2ŌłÆ2 = 444.
Case 2: There are six possible pairs.
For pairs [2,3][2, 3][2,3] and [3,2][3, 2][3,2], answer = 2ŌłŚ3+max(2ŌłÆ3,3ŌłÆ2)2 * 3 + max(2 - 3, 3 - 2)2ŌłŚ3+max(2ŌłÆ3,3ŌłÆ2) = 777
For pairs [3,5][3, 5][3,5] and [5,3][5, 3][5,3] answer = 5ŌłŚ3+max(3ŌłÆ5,5ŌłÆ3)5 * 3 + max(3 - 5, 5 - 3)5ŌłŚ3+max(3ŌłÆ5,5ŌłÆ3) = 171717 and
For the pairs [2,5][2, 5][2,5] and [5,2][5, 2][5,2], answer = 2ŌłŚ5+max(2ŌłÆ5,5ŌłÆ2)2 * 5 + max(2 - 5, 5 - 2)2ŌłŚ5+max(2ŌłÆ5,5ŌłÆ2) = 131313.
So final answer is maximum of {7,17,13}\{7, 17, 13\}{7,17,13} = 171717.

solution.approach:
For a pair (a,b) the expression can be written as a*b + |a-b|. If we assume a >= b, the value becomes a*b + a - b = a*(b+1) - b. For a fixed a, the expression is monotonic in b: it increases with b when a > 1, is constant when a = 1, and decreases with b when a < 1. Consequently the optimum will be obtained by using extreme values of the array ŌĆō the largest positives or the smallest (most negative) numbers. It is sufficient to examine all pairs formed by the three smallest and the three largest elements (after sorting). This set contains at most 6 numbers, so checking every unordered pair among them (at most 15 pairs) yields the answer. The algorithm per test case:
1. Read N and the array.
2. Sort the array.
3. Collect the first three and the last three elements (if N < 6, just take all elements).
4. Iterate over all unordered pairs of the collected elements, compute val = a*b + llabs(a-b) using 64ŌĆæbit integers, and keep the maximum.
5. Output the maximum.
The sorting step dominates the complexity: O(N log N) time and O(N) memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (sorting). The pair checking part is O(1).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array (can be O(1) extra besides the input).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        arr.sort()
        cand = arr[:3] + arr[-3:]
        best = -10**30
        m = len(cand)
        for i in range(m):
            a = cand[i]
            for j in range(i+1, m):
                b = cand[j]
                val = a * b + abs(a - b)
                if val > best:
                    best = val
        out_lines.append(str(best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
